system.notes:
Type: Antidote · Strength (drug power rating, design system pg.124): +3 · Difficulty (to synthesize): 13 · Cost: 650eb · Duration: 1D6+1 turns.

Syncomp is a broad spectrum poison antidote, used to treat nerve and biotoxins. REF is reduced at the rate of 1 point per dose.

Wiring note (Cyberpunk 2020 2nd ed. pg.122): two effects wired — (1) +3 to Poison/Toxin Saves, the Effects-Table derivation (“Antidote (+1 to Save per Strength)” at Strength 3; the printed Difficulty even reconciles: 15 Antidote + 3 Strength − 5 Reduced-REF = 13). The module's rollBoost labels are advisory display text, not auto-folded into rolls — the same mechanism as every Stun-Saves boost. (2) REF −1, the entry's printed “REF is reduced at the rate of 1 point per dose” (the Effects-Table Reduced-REF side effect: per dose, for the dose's duration, cumulative if re-dosed). Duration is in combat TURNS, so the round-tick timer counts it down. Addiction = universal core save (1D10 > Body Type). Manufacturer: not printed.

mechDrug.note: Broad-spectrum antidote: +3 to poison/toxin saves (Effects Table, Strength 3); REF -1 per dose.
